name: Quiet Days along the Dessarin
author: Leaontar Dlarvin
description:
Leaontar Dlarvin records slow seasons on the river — empty quays, gossip in riverside taverns, and the small rituals that keep bargemen sane between cargoes.

The prose is gentle and observational: herons in the reeds, children fishing off piers, and the particular silence that falls when winter ice begins to creep south from the Surbrin.
